name: Céllecs Prehistoric Route
name_original: Bosque Encantado de Orrius, Dolmen de Céllecs, Ermita Sant Bartomeu – Ruta Circular
description: Hike the moderate 6.3-mile Bosque Encantado de Orrius circular route, exploring an enchanted forest, ancient dolmen, and historic hermitage.
region_intro: Hiking around Òrrius offers diverse landscapes, from significant natural features to historical attractions. The region is characterized by interesting rock formations like the Rocas de Boquet, which provide expansive views. Hikers can explore an enchanted forest, known as Bosque Encantado de Orrius, and encounter ancient dolmens along the trails. Situated near the Parc de la Serralada Litoral, Òrrius provides a variety of popular hiking trails suitable for different fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Òrrius The most popular hiking route is Céllecs Prehistoric Route, a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 0 minutes to complete. This route explores historical sites and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Iberian settlement and Cèllecs viewpoint — circular via the Ermita Sant Bartomeu de Cabanyes, an easy 4.1 miles (6.6 km) path. This circular trail offers scenic viewpoints and passes a historic hermitage. Local hikers also love the Montaña de Cellecs and laberinto de piedras de Cabrils — circular desde Òrrius, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes. Hiking around Òrrius is defined by ancient dolmens, the enchanted forest, and the Rocas de Boquet. The network of trails offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Òrrius are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Òrrius's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Òrrius?
A: Òrrius offers a wide selection of routes, with over 20 hiking trails to explore, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and some more challenging options.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on hikes in Òrrius?
A: Hiking in Òrrius allows you to discover diverse natural features. You can explore the unique rock formations of the Rocas de Boquet, which offer expansive views, or wander through the magical setting of the 'enchanted forest' (Bosque Encantado de Orrius). The area is also rich in ancient dolmens, adding a historical layer to the natural beauty.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Òrrius?
A: Yes, Òrrius has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350705477/iberian-settlement-and-cellecs-viewpoint-circular-via-the-ermita-sant-bartomeu-de-cabanyes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iberian settlement and Cèllecs viewpoint — circular via the Ermita Sant Bartomeu de Cabanyes&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.1-mile (6.6 km) path that offers scenic viewpoints and passes a historic hermitage.

Q: What historical or cultural attractions can be found along the hiking routes?
A: Beyond natural beauty, Òrrius's trails often lead to historical and cultural sites. You can encounter ancient dolmens, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1763023" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of the Roca d'en Toni&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5150804" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Céllecs&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e945238036/cellecs-prehistoric-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Céllecs Prehistoric Route&lt;/a&gt;, are specifically designed to explore these historical sites, including an Iberian settlement and a historic hermitage.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Òrrius?
A: Yes, Òrrius features several popular circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1326101006/orrius-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Òrrius — circular route&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, offering a comprehensive experience of the local landscape. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350721603/montana-de-cellecs-and-laberinto-de-piedras-de-cabrils-circular-desde-orrius" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montaña de Cellecs and laberinto de piedras de Cabrils — circular desde Òrrius&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through varied landscapes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Òrrius?
A: The hiking experience in Òrrius is highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 170 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the presence of ancient dolmens, the enchanting forest, and the scenic Rocas de Boquet, highlighting the area's blend of natural beauty and historical intrigue.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or summits to reach on the trails?
A: Yes, the region offers several points with excellent views. You can find viewpoints along routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350705477/iberian-settlement-and-cellecs-viewpoint-circular-via-the-ermita-sant-bartomeu-de-cabanyes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iberian settlement and Cèllecs viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. For higher vantage points, consider exploring nearby summits such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3528179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Creu de Montcabrer Summit&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3240737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó d'Onofre Arnau&lt;/a&gt;, which provide expansive panoramas.

Q: Is Òrrius suitable for dog-friendly hiking?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on trails can vary, the natural and varied terrain of Òrrius generally makes it a welcoming area for hiking with dogs. Many trails pass through forests and open spaces, offering a great environment for your canine companion. Always ensure your dog is under control and check for any local signage regarding pet access.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Òrrius?
A: Òrrius is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with blooming wildflowers or colorful foliage. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended. Winter provides cooler, crisp air, and trails are generally accessible, though conditions can vary.

Q: Are there any water features or springs along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, you can find natural water sources in the area. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3896499" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Gurguí Spring&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3896551" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font d'en Mamet&lt;/a&gt;. These springs offer refreshing stops and add to the natural charm of the trails.

Q: How can I get to the hiking trails in Òrrius using public transport?
A: While Òrrius is a small municipality, public transport options exist to reach the area. It's advisable to check local bus schedules from nearby larger towns like Argentona or Mataró, which may have connections to Òrrius. Planning your route in advance using public transport apps or local transport websites is recommended.

Q: Where can I park my car when visiting Òrrius for hiking?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the village of Òrrius. Depending on your chosen starting point for a hike, you may find designated parking areas or roadside spots. It's always a good idea to arrive early, especially on weekends, to secure a convenient parking space near popular trailheads.
tips: Here you can find the moai (characteristic of Easter Island), the Indian (an American Indian, a man) and the elephant carved in rock. They are very close to the car park, for that reason it is an excursion suitable for everyone and does not require any type of physical preparation.

This interesting archaeological site is located in the forest of the municipality of La Roca del Vallés. It belongs to the Popular Neolithic period. It is moderately preserved because it does not present all its original structure.

   It is a small gallery. It preserves a rectangular chamber with a cover and a slab on the left side of its access corridor. The chamber is oriented to the southwest and it is believed that the original corridor must have been about 2 m long and the mound could reach 8 m in diameter. Two aspects of this construction could be highlighted, one is its location, fully dominating the Pre-coastal Depression, which indicates that it could be seen from many places, and the other is the orientation axis of the chamber, with the entrance to the southwest, made little usual.

The church of Sant Bartomeu de Cabanyes is located about two kilometers from the town of Orrius, belonging to the Maresme region in the province of Barcelona.

   It is a simple 11th-12th century Romanesque construction, consisting of a nave covered with a barrel vault and a semicircular apse with a small central window. It has a single-hollow belfry bell tower. During the Spanish civil war all his images were burned.

faqs.question: Is this circular route suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: The route is generally considered moderate in difficulty. However, sections within the Stone Sculptures of El Bosque Encantado are relatively easy, making it a popular and engaging option for families. The unique rock formations add an element of fun for all ages.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along this hike?
faqs.answer: This route is packed with unique sights! You'll explore the mystical Stone Sculptures of El Bosque Encantado, where natural rock formations resemble figures like an elephant, an Indian face, and a Moai statue. Further along, you'll encounter the ancient Dolmen de Céllecs, a prehistoric megalithic structure, and the historic Sant Bartomeu de Cabanyes Hermitage with its intricate stone architecture.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: While the exact starting point can vary, many hikers begin near the village of Orrius. Look for designated parking areas in or around Orrius, which provides convenient access to the Bosque Encantado and the start of the circular route.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. It is located within the Parc de la Serralada Litoral. While dogs are welcome, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially in areas where wildlife might be present or near other hikers, to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone and comply with park regulations.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn are often ideal. The weather is milder, and the natural scenery is particularly vibrant. Summer can be hot, so an early start is recommended, while winter offers a different, often quieter, experience.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this route or to access the Parc de la Serralada Litoral.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, offering a mix of comfortable walking paths, especially within the enchanted forest. You'll encounter natural rock formations and some sections with moderate inclines and declines. The path is often well-signposted, making navigation straightforward.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trail?
faqs.answer: The route passes near the village of Orrius, where you might find some basic amenities like cafes or small shops. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, as facilities directly on the trail are limited.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that are part of official trails such as Granollers-La Roca del Vallès, Ruta Prehistòrica I : Can Gol-Céllecs, and El Camí IT7TR2 El Camí de Llevant: de Montserrat a Salses, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any specific tips for navigating the Enchanted Forest of Orrius?
faqs.answer: When exploring the Stone Sculptures of El Bosque Encantado, keep a keen eye out! Many of the unique rock formations are scattered throughout the forest and sometimes require careful observation to spot amidst the vegetation. Take your time to fully appreciate the 'magical' atmosphere and discover all the hidden figures.

waypoints.name: Stone Sculptures of El Bosque Encantado
